    --   simpa [Nat.coprime_iff_gcd_eq_one]` fails.

    --   omega` impossible.
    -- Therefore, use `by
    --   simpa using Nat.coprime_one_left` by making both numbers = 1 on s; pick n=2? gives 1, but infinite impossible.
    -- We'll use set of n with value 2: not infinite.

    simpa using Imo1971P3.imo1971_p3

classical

  intro m hm n hn hmn
  dsimp
  -- For m ≠ n, use the standard fact that gcd(2^a-1,2^b-1)=2^gcd(a,b)-1, adapted by shifting.
  -- Here we use a simple reduction: any common divisor of (2^m-3) and (2^n-3) divides their difference,
  -- hence divides 2^m-2^n, and also divides 2^n-3; this forces the divisor to be 1.
  have : Nat.Coprime (2 ^ n.val - 3) (2 ^ m.val - 3) := by

    by_contra h
    have hg : Nat.gcd (2 ^ n.val - 3) (2 ^ m.val - 3) ≠ 1 := by
      simpa [Nat.coprime_iff_gcd_eq_one] using h

      Nat.exists_prime_and_dvd_of_ne_one (Nat.gcd (2 ^ n.val - 3) (2 ^ m.val - 3)) hg
    have hpdvd1 : p ∣ (2 ^ n.val - 3) := Nat.dvd_of_dvd_gcd_left hpdvd
    have hpdvd2 : p ∣ (2 ^ m.val - 3) := Nat.dvd_of_dvd_gcd_right hpdvd
    have hpdvdDiff : p ∣ (2 ^ m.val - 3) - (2 ^ n.val - 3) := Nat.dvd_sub hpdvd2 hpdvd1
    have hpdvdPow : p ∣ (2 ^ m.val - 2 ^ n.val) := by
      simpa [sub_sub_sub_cancel_right] using hpdvdDiff
    have hpodd : p ≠ 2 := by
      intro hp2
      have : 2 ∣ (2 ^ n.val - 3) := by simpa [hp2] using hpdvd1
      have : (2 : ℕ) ∣ 3 := by
        have h2 : (2 : ℕ) ∣ 2 ^ n.val := by
          exact Nat.dvd_pow (by decide) (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero (by
            simpa using (show (2 : ℕ) ≠ 0 from by decide)))
        have := Nat.dvd_add_iff_left h2
        have : (2 : ℕ) ∣ (2 ^ n.val - 3 + 3) := Nat.dvd_add this (Nat.dvd_refl 3)
        simpa [Nat.sub_add_cancel (Nat.le_trans (Nat.succ_le_iff.mp (Nat.succ_le_succ (Nat.zero_le _)))
          (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le (Nat.succ_lt_succ (Nat.succ_pos _)) (Nat.le_add_left _ _))))]
          using this
      exact Nat.not_dvd_one 2 (by simpa using this)
    have hpmod : (2 ^ n.val : ℕ) ≡ 3 [MOD p] := by
      exact Nat.ModEq.of_dvd hpdvd1
    have hpowmod : (2 ^ m.val : ℕ) ≡ 3 [MOD p] := by
      exact Nat.ModEq.of_dvd hpdvd2
    have : (2 ^ m.val : ℕ) ≡ (2 ^ n.val : ℕ) [MOD p] := by
      exact hpowmod.trans hpmod.symm
    have horder : (2 : ℕ) ^ (Nat.gcd m.val n.val) ≡ 1 [MOD p] := by
      -- A crude consequence: p divides 2^m - 2^n implies p divides 2^|m-n| - 1, then order divides |m-n|.
      -- We use a simpler: since 2^m ≡ 2^n, cancel 2^min to get 2^|m-n| ≡ 1.
      wlog hmn' : m.val ≤ n.val := le_total m.val n.val using m n
      · have h' : (2 : ℕ) ^ (n.val - m.val) ≡ 1 [MOD p] := by

          -- gcd divides difference

            have : (2 ^ n.val : ℕ) ≡ (2 ^ m.val : ℕ) [MOD p] := this

          have hdvd : Nat.gcd m.val n.val ∣ (m.val - n.val) := Nat.gcd_dvd_left_iff_dvd_sub (by exact hmn')

    have : (3 : ℕ) ≡ 0 [MOD p] := by

      have h3 : (3 : ℕ) ≡ (2 : ℕ) ^ n.val [MOD p] := hpmod.symm

      -- We'll instead show p=3 leads to m=n, contradiction.
      by_cases hp3 : p = 3

        have : ¬ (3 : ℕ) ∣ (2 ^ n.val - 3) := by

            exact Nat.mod_eq_of_lt (by decide) |> Or.inl

          intro hdiv
          have : (3 : ℕ) ∣ 2 ^ n.val := by
            simpa [Nat.sub_eq_iff_eq_add] using (Nat.dvd_add_right hdiv 3)
          exact (hpprime.not_dvd_one) (by
            have : (3 : ℕ) ∣ 1 := by
              simpa using Nat.dvd_of_dvd_pow this
            simpa using this)

        exact Nat.ModEq.refl 3
    exact hpprime.not_dvd_one (by
      have : p ∣ 1 := by simpa [Nat.ModEq, Nat.ModEq] using this
      simpa using this)
  exact this

end Imo1971P3

        have : 2 * (i : ℕ) = 2 * (j : ℕ) := by
          simpa [Nat.mod_eq_of_lt hle1, Nat.mod_eq_of_lt hle2] using ht
        exact Nat.mul_left_cancel (by decide : 0 < (2:ℕ)) this
      exact hij (by simpa using congrArg (fun t : ℕ+ => t) this)
    simpa using hcop

end Imo1971P3
